sources.title: CCR T14 §5.85(a)(1) — Trout, General Statewide Regulations (inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40 — Alphabetical List of Hatchery Trout, Hatchery Steelhead, and Salmon Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Tyler Lake not listed)

CCR T14 §7.50 — Alphabetical List of Trout Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Tyler Lake not listed; the Lake Tahoe tributaries rule (b)(152) does not apply — Tyler Lake is on the Pacific slope, not a Lake Tahoe tributary)
sources.quotedText: (152) Tahoe Lake and tributaries (Placer and El Dorado Cos.). (A) Tahoe Lake tributaries upstream to the first lake. Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout.

sources.title: 2026 California Freshwater Sport Fishing Regulations (Updated 06/01/2026)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and 10 trout possession limit.

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: 2026 Provisional Stocking Document for Trout and Inland Salmon — CDFW (Tyler Lake not listed)
Desolation Wilderness Permits — Eldorado National Forest (USDA Forest Service)
sources.quotedText: Permits are required year-round for overnight use.

sources.title: Desolation Day Use Permits & Parking — Eldorado National Forest (USDA Forest Service)
sources.quotedText: Day use is not subject to fees nor limited by quota at any time of the year.

sources.title: Golden trout in the Desolation Wilderness — Wanders With Trout (angler trip report)
sources.quotedText: I had been told that this lake contained a "modest population of small but robust goldens".

parameters.note: Tyler Lake is not listed in CCR §7.40(b) or §7.50(b), so it falls under the general statewide lake regulation in §5.85(a)(1). It is a high lake on the west slope of the Crystal Range in Desolation Wilderness (Eldorado National Forest, Wrights Lake / Tyler Lake Trail area, El Dorado Co.). It drains west to the American River watershed on the Pacific slope — not into the Lake Tahoe basin — so the Lake Tahoe tributaries special regulation §7.50(b)(152), which closes Tahoe tributaries seasonally 'upstream to the first lake,' does not reach it; the lake follows the general lake regulation.

regulations.humanSummary: Open all year (general statewide lake regulation; not listed as a special-regulation water)
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

parameters.description: Tyler Lake lies inside Desolation Wilderness (Tyler Lake / Rockbound Trailhead access near Wrights Lake). A USDA Forest Service wilderness permit is required year-round for overnight use; an overnight quota applies from the Friday before Memorial Day through September 30. Day use is free and not limited by quota. Bear canisters are required for all overnight visitors.

raw.overnight_quota_season: Friday before Memorial Day through September 30

regulations.citation: USDA Forest Service — Desolation Wilderness permit program
regulations.humanSummary: Desolation Wilderness permit: required year-round for overnight use (quota Fri. before Memorial Day–Sep. 30); day use free and un-quota'd.
regulations.verbatimText: Permits are required year-round for overnight use.

parameters.description: Fishery note. A first-person angler trip report (Wanders With Trout, 2009) describes a Desolation Wilderness lake holding a 'modest population of small but robust goldens' (golden trout, ~7-8 in); in a later comment the author identifies the lake as 'Tyler Lake on the west slope of the Crystal Range.' Golden trout are not native to this basin (they are a Kern River, southern Sierra native) and would be a remnant of historical plantings — CDFW aerially planted golden trout in a few Desolation lakes historically and has since ended aerial stocking of Desolation Wilderness lakes. CDFW does not list Tyler Lake in its 2026 Provisional Stocking plan, so any golden trout are a self-sustaining/historical population, not a current put-and-take fishery. This is a third-party angler report, not a CDFW fish survey.

regulations.citation: Wanders With Trout — 'Golden trout in the Desolation Wilderness' (identified as Tyler Lake by the author); CDFW 2026 Provisional Stocking Document (Tyler Lake not listed)
regulations.humanSummary: Reported by a third-party angler as holding a small golden-trout population; not currently stocked by CDFW.
regulations.verbatimText: I had been told that this lake contained a "modest population of small but robust goldens".
